standard_description: Use the relation i^(2) = –1 and the commutative, associative, and distributive properties to add, subtract, and multiply complex numbers.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Problem 1
Evaluate the following complex expressions:
$${(8+3i)-(6-2i)}$$
$${(8+3i)(6-2i)}$$
$${(2+i)^2+(6+2i)}$$
Problem 2
Which of the following results in a real number? Select all that apply.
$${i^2}$$
$${(2-i)(2+i)}$$
$${2i^3}$$
$${3i+2i}$$

Task
Let $z = 1 + i$ where $i^2 = -1$.   Calculate $z^2, z^3,$ and  $z^4$.
Graph $z, z^2, z^3,$ and  $z^4$ in the complex plane.  What do you notice about the positions of these  numbers?
What is $z^{100}$?  Explain.

Task
Working with complex numbers allows us to solve equations like $z^2 = -1$
which cannot be solved with real numbers.  Here we will investigate complex numbers which arise as square roots of certain complex numbers.
Find all complex square roots of -1, that is, find all numbers $z = a + bi$ which satisfy $z^2 = -1$.
Find all complex square roots of 1.
Which complex numbers satisfy $z^2 = i$?

Task
Working with complex numbers allows us to solve equations like $z^2 = -1$
which cannot be solved with real numbers.  Here we will investigate complex numbers which arise as square roots of certain complex numbers.
Find all complex square roots of -1, that is, find all numbers $z = a + bi$ which satisfy $z^2 = -1$.
Find all complex square roots of 1.
Which complex numbers satisfy $z^2 = i$?

Task
For each odd positive integer $n$, the only real number solution to $x^n = 1$ is $x = 1$ while for even positive integers $n$, $x = 1$ and $x = -1$ are solutions to $x^n = 1$. In this problem we look for all complex number solutions to $x^n = 1$ for some small values of $n$.
Find all complex numbers $a + bi$ whose cube is 1.
Find all complex numbers $a + bi$ whose fourth power is 1.

metadata.title: Complex Cube and Fourth Roots of 1

problems.text:
Problem 1
This is a complex number because it has a real part and an imaginary part:
$${{3+2i}}$$
What is the sum of
$${{3+2i}}$$
and
$${2+4i}$$
?
Problem 2
What is the product of
$${(3+2i)}$$
and
$${(2+4i)}$$
?

Task
For each odd positive integer $n$, the only real number solution to $x^n = 1$ is $x = 1$ while for even positive integers $n$, $x = 1$ and $x = -1$ are solutions to $x^n = 1$. In this problem we look for all complex number solutions to $x^n = 1$ for some small values of $n$.
Find all complex numbers $a + bi$ whose cube is 1.
Find all complex numbers $a + bi$ whose fourth power is 1.

metadata.title: Complex Cube and Fourth Roots of 1

khan_exercises.title: Add & subtract matrices
khan_exercises.description: Practice your understanding of the process of adding or subtracting two matrices.
topic_path: Topic C: Lesson 25: Properties of matrix addition

hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & -2 \\\\
0 & -2 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
7 & 1 \\\\
3 & -3 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -1 \\\\
5 & -2 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the sum:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 6 \\\\
2 & 4 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & -1 \\\\
1 & 1 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3 & -2 \\\\
-1 & 3 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & -1 \\\\
-5 & 1 
\end{array} \right]$
To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

This is the difference:

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & -1 \\\\
6 & 0 
\end{array} \right]$

items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix addition.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
x & -1 \\\\
-4 & 5 \\\\
3 & -2 
\end{array} \right]
+
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -1 \\\\
3 & y \\\\
-2 & -3 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -2 \\\\
-1 & 9 \\\\
1 & -5 
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=0
\\\\
y&=4
\end{align}$

items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix addition.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 3 \\\\
-1 & x
\end{array} \right]
+
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
y & -5 \\\\
4 & -2 
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & -2 \\\\
3 & 2
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=4
\\\\
y&=-2
\end{align}$

items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix subtraction.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3 & x & -2
\\\\
-1 & 0 & 4
\end{array} \right]
-
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-2 & 3 & 0
\\\\
5 & 1 & y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & 2 & -2
\\\\
-6 & -1 & 5
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=5
\\\\
y&=-1
\end{align}$

items.question_content:
**Find the values of $x$ and $y$ in the following matrix subtraction.**

$\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5
\\\\
x
\\\\
-1
\end{array} \right]
-
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-3
 \\\\
2
\\\\
y
\end{array} \right]
=
\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
8
\\\\
-5
\\\\
-6
\end{array} \right]$

$x=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$y=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: To add or subtract matrices that have the same dimensions, we add or subtract the corresponding terms from each matrix.

So these must be the values of $x$ and $y$:

$\begin{align}
x&=-3
\\\\
y&=5
\end{align}$

items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & -6 & 0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-3 & 2
\\\\
1 & -2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-4 & 1 & -1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
5 & 0 & -2
\\\\
-4 & 2 & 1 
\\\\
4 & -2 & 3
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold C$ are both $1\times 3$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

This is the possible calculation:

- $\bold A-\bold C$

items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & 2 & 1
\\\\
3 & 0 & 2 
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & 1
\\\\
3 & 6
\\\\
1 & 0
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2 & -1
\\\\
-1 & 0
\\\\
4 & 1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
1 & -2 & 3
\\\\
1 & 1 & 4 
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold D$ are both $2\times 3$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold B$ and $\bold C$ are both $3\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

These are the possible calculations:

- $\bold A-\bold D$
- $\bold B+\bold C$

items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3
\\\\
4
\\\\
5
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-1 & -3
\\\\
0 & 4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
2
\\\\
1
\\\\
0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-5 & 2
\\\\
3 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold C$ are both $3\times 1$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold B$ and $\bold D$ are both $2\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Other combinations are not possible.

These are the possible calculations:

- $\bold A+\bold C$
- $\bold C-\bold A$
- $\bold D-\bold B$

items.question_content:
Here are four matrices:

$\begin{align}
\bold A=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
-5
\\\\
1
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold B=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
3
\\\\
-2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
\bold C=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
0 & 0
\end{array} \right]
&\quad \bold D=\left[ \begin{array}{cc}
4 & -3
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

**Which of the following calculations are possible?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can add or subtract two matrices if they have the same dimensions. Otherwise, the operation is not possible.

Matrics $\bold A$ and $\bold B$ are both $2\times 1$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

Matrics $\bold C$ and $\bold D$ are both $1\times 2$ matrices. So we can add and subtract them.

All four calculations are possible:

- $\bold A+\bold B$
- $\bold B-\bold A$
- $\bold C-\bold D$
- $\bold D+\bold C$
